# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.

Write secure code.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

# Secure temporary file creation
TEMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d) || exit 1
trap 'rm -rf "$TEMP_DIR"' EXIT INT TERM

# Set restrictive permissions on temp directory
chmod 700 "$TEMP_DIR"

# Create secure temporary files with unpredictable names
TEMP_INPUT=$(mktemp "$TEMP_DIR/input.XXXXXXXXXX") || exit 1
TEMP_OUTPUT=$(mktemp "$TEMP_DIR/output.XXXXXXXXXX") || exit 1
TEMP_PROCESSED=$(mktemp "$TEMP_DIR/processed.XXXXXXXXXX") || exit 1

# Set restrictive permissions on temp files
chmod 600 "$TEMP_INPUT" "$TEMP_OUTPUT" "$TEMP_PROCESSED"

# Sample data processing pipeline
echo "Starting secure data processing pipeline..."

# Write initial data to temp file
cat > "$TEMP_INPUT" << 'EOF'
data1,100
data2,200
data3,300
data4,400
data5,500
EOF

# Process data: extract second column and calculate sum
echo "Processing data..."
awk -F',' '{sum += $2} END {print "Total:", sum}' "$TEMP_INPUT" > "$TEMP_OUTPUT"

# Further processing: add timestamp
echo "Adding metadata..."
{
    echo "Processed at: $(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S UTC")"
    cat "$TEMP_OUTPUT"
} > "$TEMP_PROCESSED"

# Read and display results
echo "Results:"
cat "$TEMP_PROCESSED"

# Additional processing example: count records
RECORD_COUNT=$(wc -l < "$TEMP_INPUT")
echo "Total records processed: $RECORD_COUNT"

# Cleanup is handled by trap
echo "Processing complete. Temporary files will be securely removed."
